title: Sprint 4.4 — The Canon Entry System
author: Rachael Nike · Zorya
body:
# APP-004 — Canon Entry Reader & Writer

> Entries are not documents. Entries are living knowledge objects.
> Everything inside the Canon eventually becomes an Entry.

## Objective

Build the Canon Entry System. Entries are the fundamental building blocks of the Black Poppy Canon.

- Books contain Entries.
- Projects reference Entries.
- Symbols connect Entries.
- Everything begins here.

## What shipped

| Piece | Status |
| --- | --- |
| Entry reader & writer | ✓ |
| Markdown editor + live preview | ✓ |
| Metadata panel | ✓ |
| Relationships (unlimited) | ✓ |
| Version history — nothing is deleted | ✓ |
| Autosave every 10 seconds | ✓ |
| Print layout | ✓ |
| In-entry search | ✓ |

## Acceptance criteria

- [x] Entry opens
- [x] Metadata visible
- [x] Markdown editor functional
- [x] Live preview
- [x] Responsive
- [x] Autosave
- [x] Print layout
- [x] Relationship panel
- [x] Mock data only

## Definition of done

The Canon can now be written.

versions.summary: Entry created from the approved APP-004 specification.
versions.author: Zorya

title: How to write an Entry
author: Zorya
body:
# How to write an Entry

This page is alive. Press **Write** above and change it — the Canon will remember every version.

## The essentials

- **Write / Read** — switch between the editor and the finished page.
- **Save** (`Ctrl+S`) — every explicit save creates a version. Add a one-line summary so future-you knows what changed.
- **Autosave** — your draft is kept safe every ten seconds, quietly.
- **Print** (`Ctrl+P`) — the entry prints like a journal page.
- **Find** (`Ctrl+K`) — search inside this entry.

## What the editor speaks

Markdown, plainly:

```
# Heading
**bold** and *italic*
> a quotation
- a list
- [ ] a task not yet done
| tables | too |
| --- | --- |
```

## Relationships

Every Entry may reach toward Books, Projects, Symbols, Companions, Products, and other Entries. Add them in the Relationships panel below the page. There is no limit — the Canon is a root system, not a filing cabinet.

## Nothing is deleted

Entries archive; they do not die. Every saved version stays in the Version History, and any old version can be restored — which simply writes a new version. The record only grows.

---

*Noli illegitimi carborundum.*
